last_week_digest.title: Iran-linked group Handala hacked FBI Director Kash Patel’s personal email account
last_week_digest.tldr: Iran-linked group Handala hacked FBI Director Kash Patel's personal email, leaking files, with the FBI confirming no government data was exposed, offering $10 million for information on the hackers, and stressing the leaked material is historical, involving no classified information, and largely dating back to 2019.

last_week_digest.title: European Commission confirms data breach after Europa.eu hack
last_week_digest.tldr: The European Commission confirmed a data breach after its Europa.eu platform was hacked, with over 350 GB of data stolen, including employee information and sensitive documents, affecting at least one AWS account, and prompting notification of potentially affected Union entities.

last_week_digest.title: Russia-linked APT TA446 uses DarkSword exploit to target iPhone users in phishing wave
last_week_digest.tldr: Russia-linked APT TA446 is using the DarkSword exploit kit to target iPhone users in phishing campaigns, compromising devices and highlighting a growing threat from state-sponsored actors, primarily targeting NATO countries, defense, and intelligence consulting companies, with no previous attacks on Apple devices, now enabling credential harvesting and intelligence collection.

last_week_digest.title: AFC Ajax drops ball as flaws let hackers play admin with tickets and bans
last_week_digest.tldr: Dutch football club AFC Ajax suffered a data breach, exposing data of 300,000 supporters and 42,000 season tickets, after a hacker exploited vulnerabilities, accessing and modifying accounts, and lifting stadium bans, with potential career harm for affected individuals, such as a local government worker.

last_week_digest.title: FBI Confirms Kash Patel Email Hack as US Offers $10M Reward for Hackers
last_week_digest.tldr: Iranian hackers targeted Kash Patel's personal email, compromising old information, the FBI confirmed, as the US offers a $10M reward for the hackers, affecting the director's personal data, with potential operational consequences, signalling a significant cybersecurity threat to high-profile individuals and organisations globally.

last_week_digest.title: China-Linked groups target Southeast Asian government with advanced malware in 2025
last_week_digest.tldr: A Southeast Asian government was targeted by China-linked groups in 2025, with three clusters deploying multiple malware families, including HIUPAN and PUBLOAD, in a sophisticated cyber campaign, demonstrating advanced tactics and persistent access to sensitive systems, with significant overlap in tactics and techniques.

last_week_digest.title: TeamPCP’s attack spree slows, but threat escalates with ransomware pivot
last_week_digest.tldr: TeamPCP's attack spree has slowed, but the threat escalates with a ransomware pivot, partnering with Vect, a new RaaS operation, affecting companies compromised in previous supply chain attacks, with one confirmed deployment using TeamPCP-sourced credentials, signalling a shift from expansion to monetization, with 300 GB of stolen credentials potentially enabling future compromises.

last_week_digest.title: Google Drive now detects ransomware and helps restore affected files
last_week_digest.tldr: Google Drive now detects ransomware, alerting users and admins, and helps restore affected files, with its AI model detecting 14× more infections, protecting organisations from malware attacks, with files modified within 25 days restorable, minimising business disruption and data loss.

last_week_digest.title: Russian APT Star Blizzard Adopts DarkSword iOS Exploit Kit
last_week_digest.tldr: Russian APT group Star Blizzard has adopted the DarkSword iOS exploit kit, targeting government, education, finance, and legal entities globally, affecting various sectors and potentially thousands of users, with significant operational consequences for the impacted organisations, signalling a growing threat to mobile security in the industry, with widespread implications.

last_week_digest.title: Telnyx joins LiteLLM in latest PyPI package poisoning tied to Trivy breach
last_week_digest.tldr: Telnyx's Python SDK on PyPI was compromised, with 34,000 weekly downloads potentially exposed to credential-stealing malware, affecting developers and services that installed malicious package versions 4.87.1 or 4.87.2, prompting users to treat environments as compromised and rotate exposed credentials.

last_week_digest.title: Pro-Russian hackers pose as Ukraine's cyber agency to target government, businesses
last_week_digest.tldr: Pro-Russian hackers targeted Ukrainian government agencies and businesses, impersonating the national cyber agency in a phishing campaign, affecting a small number of devices, mainly in educational institutions, with roughly one million users potentially exposed, and over 200,000 devices allegedly compromised, resulting in limited operational disruption.

last_week_digest.title: TeamPCP Explores Ways to Exploit Stolen Supply Chain Secrets
last_week_digest.tldr: TeamPCP, linked to extortion group Lapsus$, is exploiting stolen supply chain secrets, including cloud credentials and SSH keys, to monetize harvested data, potentially affecting over a third of cloud environments, and partnering with Vect ransomware group to deploy ransomware across compromised companies.

last_week_digest.title: Nearly half a Million mobile customers of Lloyds Banking Group affected by security incident
last_week_digest.tldr: Lloyds Banking Group's faulty software update exposed transaction details of nearly 450,000 mobile banking users, affecting 447,936 customers who saw other users' transactions, with 114,182 clicking on them, but no financial loss occurred, and £139,000 compensation was paid to 3,625 customers for distress.

last_week_digest.title: Financial groups lay out a plan to fight AI identity attacks
last_week_digest.tldr: Financial groups are combating AI identity attacks, which increased 700% in 2023, with projected losses of $40 billion by 2027. A joint paper calls for policymakers to act, citing 42% of Suspicious Activity Reports tied to identity compromise, and recommends phishing-resistant authentication and identity proofing initiatives to mitigate the growing threat.

last_week_digest.title: Axios NPM Package Breached in North Korean Supply Chain Attack
last_week_digest.tldr: A North Korean supply chain attack breached the Axios NPM package, exploiting a long-lived access token to push backdoored versions, potentially affecting thousands of users, with significant business consequences for affected companies, highlighting the growing threat of global supply chain attacks on the software development industry.

last_week_digest.title: TrueConf zero-day vulnerability exploited to target government networks
last_week_digest.tldr: Suspected China-nexus attackers exploited a TrueConf zero-day vulnerability to target Southeast Asian government networks, distributing malware via a compromised update mechanism, affecting an unspecified number of users, with potential significant operational consequences, signalling increased risk for videoconferencing platforms used by governments and critical infrastructure operators worldwide.

last_week_digest.title: Mercor confirms security incident tied to LiteLLM supply chain attack
last_week_digest.tldr: Mercor, a $10 billion-valued AI recruiting platform, confirmed a security incident tied to the LiteLLM supply chain attack, affecting thousands of firms, with hacking gang Lapsus$ claiming to have obtained hundreds of gigabytes of Mercor's data, prompting an investigation and remediation efforts.

last_week_digest.title: Google links Axios npm supply chain attack to North Korea-linked APT UNC1069
last_week_digest.tldr: Google attributed the Axios npm supply chain attack to North Korea-linked APT UNC1069, targeting 100M weekly downloads, with potential exposure to ~400M monthly downloads, allowing malware to spread through a trusted library used by millions of projects, compromising developers and organizations.

last_week_digest.title: Chinese Hackers Target European Governments in Espionage Campaigns
last_week_digest.tldr: Chinese state-backed group TA416 targeted European governments in cyber espionage campaigns, with Proofpoint researchers detecting renewed activity in mid-2025, affecting EU and NATO diplomatic missions, and expanding to Middle Eastern entities in March 2026, altering infection chains and using custom PlugX payload, signaling a significant threat to global government and diplomatic security.

last_week_digest.title: Linx Security Raises $50 Million for Identity Security and Governance
last_week_digest.tldr: Linx Security has raised $50 million to enhance identity security and governance, accelerating product development and expanding globally, which will likely strengthen its market position and improve customer protection, ultimately benefiting businesses worldwide with enhanced security measures and broader market reach.
